zone_6b_notes: Direct sow when soil reaches 60°F — May 1–15 in Farmington. Must be planted in blocks (minimum 4 rows) not single rows — corn is wind-pollinated and isolated plants produce poor, gappy ears. Missouri summers are ideal for sweet corn; harvest when silk turns brown and kernels are milky. Isolation required if growing supersweet varieties near regular sweet corn.
water: 1–1.5 inches per week — especially critical at tassel and silking stage
